content: Hey @N0_R3M0RS3, I notice that your Input section states that the input will contain the solid rectangle, while the Output section requires only a single integer representing the number of characters on the rectangle perimeter. However, the example section shows that the solid rectangle appears in the Output section instead. Could you clarify whether you only require players to output the perimeter or if you want the solid rectangle as well?

❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: Input and output specifications don't match cases.

Worth noting that there are a few related but not too similar clashes that cover the same topic:
- https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/66611be2e83344fbd5d1a32f07d07fd71884
- https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/141486711b53b9f488c9e6ea7be5fc57c52e9e

In my opinion, this clash is sufficiently unique.
I'm really confused about your input section because it doesn't match your stub generator and test cases. Please fix (sorry if you were doing that already)

The "noise" characters mentioned in the Goal section might be referring to the irrelevant descriptions 😅

A few additional areas to fix, on top of what @VinceMC already pointed out:

Goal / Input / Output
* There's no need to repeat "Goal", "Input" and "Output" within the descriptions. Please remove those headings.

Formatting
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement (including the full example there), and format your statement properly as described in the points below.

Input
* Please add "space-separated" before "integers".

* Please format all line numbers in <<bold>> (e.g. <<Line 1>>).

Input / Constraints
* Please format all [[variables]] (e.g. [[W]]).

* Please keep variable names consistent throughout different sections. Use either [[W]] and [[H]], or [[w]] and [[h]].

Cases
* Please modify Validator 2 to use the same inputs as Test 2 because 1 1 is an edge case.

* Please add a new pair of cases where W < H.

Thanks.

Just to clarify, when you say "unique", are you referring to the requirement to use a specified character to draw the rectangle, or the requirement to output the perimeter?

If it's the former, it's a small distinction but I'm still fine to treat that unique. If it's the latter, though, my concern is that it effectively mixes two tasks into a single clash, which I'm not convinced is the right approach.

Also, if you have a moment, would you mind joining the discussion in the thread below? I'm trying to guide the author toward identifying the issues and possible solutions themselves, so I've been avoiding providing the fixes directly.

oh yeah when doing the stub thing i couldnt figure out how to make the rectangle so i just made it to where you had to make it in the output and i forgot to change the input and output statements
Hmm ok, so is the solid rectangle output your intention? If you want it in the Input section instead I can help you with that.

well after hearing what @5DN1L said I realised that that would be too much for one clash so instead im ttaking out the rectangle all together creating a new where you make a rectangle

im making it right now but when im done im gonna remove the rectangle stuff from here
i just completeted the second one now im going to work on this one
Hey @N0_R3M0RS3, computing the perimeter from the width and height of a rectangle seems more like a math problem than a programming problem... this might not be suitable as a clash.

I almost wanted to reject the idea when I first saw it, but the edge case helps introduce a small element of programming (use if/else or min)… unless a different single formula exists to pass all cases.
I meant the latter (I assume you mean perimeter); your concern makes sense also.
Oops. Yup, my bad. Corrected.
I notice you used "parameter" in the other thread as well :) Let me clarify the author's intentions in a new thread.
Thank you so much for pointing out that embarrassing typo 😂 I made the same mistake because I drafted both messages around the same time.

Actually, I'm starting to understand your clash better, and it's similar to a tortoise vs hare racetrack clash that I've played before. Maybe if you add this in your goal section, it can make your puzzle easier to understand:
<<Example:>>
```
ooooo   Width: 5
o---o   Height: 3
ooooo   Characters: 12
```
Adding an example would be good, but it's also important for the author to rewrite the existing statement and remove everything that's irrelevant to the task.
ngl this is my first ever one but what does the <<>> mean because you said something about lines that i didnt understand
nevermind i figured it out
also can you help me cause on my last validator its supposed to be outputting 100 but instead it outputs 102 and i couldnt publish it until i changed its output to 102

Your last validator does expect the output to be 100; I guess you've figured it out already.

Goal
* Please remove the extra "Goal:" heading.

* The Goal section mentions that the rectangle is surrounded by noise characters. Why is this mentioned when the example illustration doesn't show any noise characters, and the input for the test cases doesn't include any information about them?

Input
* The Input section says there are (H + 2) lines of input. Why is that the case if all the provided cases currently contain only one line of input?

Output
* The Output section says we should count the number of characters [[C]]. Why is [[C]] referenced when no such character is provided in the input?

Constraints
* Please remove the constraint [[W]] < [[H]]. I'd only suggested adding some *cases* where [[W]] < [[H]]; not introducing it as a constraint.

Cases
* Please modify Validator 2 to use the same inputs as Test 2 because 1 1 is an edge case.

* Please add a new pair of cases where W < H.

Looking over everything, do you think the statement is currently quite inconsistent? It was actually the first point raised by @VinceMC, in case it was missed 😉

Thanks.

working on it now


also can you explain to me what the validators even do like what ar ethey there for

Main differences between tests and validators:

(a) Tests are visible to players but validators are hidden.

(b) Tests are meant to help players verify their solutions before submission, while validators are used to assess the submitted code.

Each test and its validator should be similar, otherwise:

(a) A scenario covered in the validators only but not in the tests may catch players off guard.

(b) A scenario covered in the tests only but not in the validators may make the evaluation inadequate.

Hope this clarifies. 🙂

kinda but what do you mean asses the code like to make sure that they got the code right instead of just getting the test case right by luck


also this is my finished product can you give me some last minute tips
Yes. If validators are the same as tests, a player could simply hardcode a solution that passes those specific cases.
ohh that makes sense
It's bedtime for me now; I'll take a look at the revised contribution tomorrow.
alsr dont let the bed bugs bite

It seems there's still some confusion about how the Input and Output sections should correspond to the cases. Also, I'm not entirely convinced that combining two tasks (finding the perimeter and drawing the rectangle) within a single clash is the best approach.

Could you please set the state of your contribution to WIP for now and address these issues? Once everything has been resolved, you can change it back to public. Thanks.

Because two tasks in the same clash isn't a good idea. You can split this into two clashes.
